summary: Summary: Speaker2 (Carla) arrives at the beach excited, asking about timing and plans, while Speaker1 responds neutral then shows surprise as problems emerge. Early on Speaker2 becomes frustrated and then angry when she realizes she forgot her flashlight, triggering a brief, mutual exchange of frustration over possession. As they settle for the grunion run, Speaker1 expresses sadness and anger about being cold and not bringing essentials (blanket), rejecting offers and staying frustrated, while Speaker2 remains excited and reassuring, offering a jacket and pointing out the spectacle. When the fish begin to run, Speaker2 grows even more excited, loudly directing attention and celebrating the scene; Speaker1 alternates between frustration and anger but gradually shares in moments of happiness watching the event. The conversation thus moves from initial excitement to conflict and coldness, and concludes with Speaker2 excited and Speaker1 experiencing mixed emotions—some lingering frustration and emerging happiness.
